problem: Implement channel formation detection logic and center definition calculation for Human Design bodygraphs - CRITICAL foundation blocking ALL multi-chart work
goal: Produce production-ready channel formation system at src/human_design/models/channel.py with ChannelDefinition model, get_formed_channels() logic, RawBodyGraph.active_channels computed property, and comprehensive test suite

existing_patterns: src/human_design/models/core.py - GateNumber, CenterName, Planet Literal types
channels.yaml - 36 channels as gate pairs with names
architectural_foundation.calculation_logic: Two gates activate a channel when BOTH gates are activated in a bodygraph (individual or composite). Channels connect centers. Centers are defined when they have at least one active channel.
architectural_foundation.raw_vs_semantic: Channel formation is RAW calculation - independent of any interpretation system

from_review_strand_d5a05df6.blocking_for: ALL multi-chart work (interaction, penta, transit overlays)

models: ChannelDefinition(channel_id, gate_a, gate_b, name, center_a, center_b, circuit)
technical_requirements.algorithm: Iterate all 36 channels, check if both gates present in activations, collect matches

constraints: Work in strand fork space (automatic isolation branch)
Follow RawBodyGraph architectural pattern (Pydantic v2, computed properties)
Use existing GateNumber, CenterName Literal types from core.py
Maintain raw vs semantic separation (no 64keys content in models)
100% test coverage for channel detection logic
success_criteria: ChannelDefinition model with all fields typed
ChannelRegistry singleton loads all 36 channels from channels.yaml
CenterRegistry maps all 64 gates to their centers
Comprehensive pytest suite with parametrized tests
Edge cases tested: zero channels, all channels, partial activations
Validates against known bodygraphs (compare to 64keys data)
mypy --strict passes on all new code
Zero breaking changes to existing RawBodyGraph API

1_architecture_design.description: Design ChannelDefinition model and registry pattern

tasks.task: Design ChannelDefinition Pydantic model, ChannelRegistry singleton, CenterRegistry gate→center lookup. Specify computed property integration with RawBodyGraph. Reference: bodygraph.py patterns, channels.yaml structure, centers.yaml structure.

2_implementation.description: Implement channel models and integrate with RawBodyGraph

tasks.task: Implement src/human_design/models/channel.py with ChannelDefinition, ChannelRegistry, CenterRegistry. Add RawBodyGraph.active_channels and RawBodyGraph.defined_centers computed properties. Follow Pydantic v2 patterns, use Literal types, maintain type safety.

3_test_suite.description: Build comprehensive test suite for channel detection

tasks.task: Create tests/test_channel_formation.py with pytest.mark.parametrize patterns. Test cases: known bodygraphs, edge cases (zero/all channels), gate pairs, center definition. Validate against 64keys data for accuracy.

4_validation.description: Validate implementation against existing patterns and requirements

tasks.task: Audit channel formation implementation for architectural consistency, type safety, test coverage, edge case handling. Validate computed properties don't break existing RawBodyGraph usage. Check mypy --strict compliance.

5_synthesis.description: Synthesize implementation notes and integration guide

tasks.task: Synthesize complete implementation notes documenting: channel formation algorithm, integration points, test strategy, known limitations, next steps (InteractionChart integration). Include usage examples and API reference.

structural: All models use Pydantic v2 BaseModel
semantic: Channel gate pairs match channels.yaml exactly
Channel detection: both gates required, no partial matches
Center definition: at least one active channel required
testing: Edge cases: zero channels, all channels, single gate
Test both active_channels and defined_centers properties

metadata.isolation: automatic git fork and branch creation
